reasoning_summary: A 52 ns full-depth Rabi pulse gives a repeated post-pulse readout dip near 3.920-3.925 GHz.
A near-pi 52 ns modulated Rabi pulse produces a localized post-pulse fluorescence dip near 3.905 GHz.
The active post-pi-pulse readout shows a localized negative contrast dip near 3.895 GHz.
A localized post-microwave readout drop near 3.885 GHz is consistent with a near-pi Rabimodulated pulse at mod_depth 1.
A near-pi mod_depth 1 pulse produces a modest signal/reference dip on the high-frequency side.
The near-pi post-pulse readout shows a localized dip below the mS=0 reference around 3.885-3.890 GHz.
A repeatable driven-readout dip near 3.880 GHz is consistent with resonance for the 52 ns mod_depth 1 Rabi pulse.
The post-pulse readout shows a coherent dip versus the 0-reference near 3.845 GHz for the 52 ns mod_depth 1 Rabi pulse.
A localized post-pulse signal dip near 3.875 GHz is consistent with a 52 ns near-pi Rabi pulse at mod_depth 1.
The near-pi MW-pulsed readout shows a localized dip below the m_S=0 reference around 3.90 GHz.
The post-pulse readout has a negative-going dip near 3.904 GHz with the expected sign and order of magnitude for the 52 ns mod_depth 1 pulse.
A local post-pulse readout dip near 3.875 GHz relative to the reference readout indicates a pODMR resonance.
A near-pi modulated pulse gives a reproducible readout-2 drop near 3.860 GHz relative to the m_S=0 reference.
The post-pulse readout shows a localized differential dip near 3.880 GHz under a near-pi Rabi pulse sequence.
A near-pi 52 ns modulated pulse at mod_depth 1 produces a localized repeatable post-pulse readout drop versus the bright reference.
Near 3.875 GHz the post-microwave readout is consistently suppressed relative to the bright reference for a near-pi Rabimodulated pulse.
A localized readout-2 dip relative to the m_S=0 reference appears near 3.90-3.905 GHz with the expected sign for the 52 ns near-pi pulse.
The post-Rabi readout shows a localized dip near 3.895 GHz with a 52 ns mod_depth 1 pulse, consistent with a pODMR resonance.
A localized post-pulse readout dip near 3.875 GHz is consistent with the near-pi Rabimodulated pulse condition.
A near-pi modulated pulse produces a modest but correctly signed post-pulse fluorescence dip versus the zero-reference readout.
A 52 ns mod_depth 1 pulse should be near pi, and the post-pulse readout shows a repeatable localized depletion near 3.845 GHz.
